title: Weekly Trading Report — Week 4 (Feb 20-27, 2026)
summary:
Overall Performance: +$435 net P&L across 10 completed trades (60% win rate). CSP strategy broke even at -$179 after NU and NVCR losses offset strong wins on DELL, NFLX, and CELH. The standout trade was the NFLX $90 call calendar spread at +$937 in one day.

Key Findings

CSP core strategy tested by three significant losses (NU -$939, NVCR -$1,334, RKLB -$632). NFLX calendar spread was the week's best trade (+$937, +79.4% return in 1 day). DELL CSP expired worthless for full $987 premium capture. VIX declining from 20+ to 17.9 confirms the regime shift predicted in Week 1.

Cumulative Performance (Feb 9-27)

20 total trades | 70% win rate | +$2,193 cumulative P&L | $52,207 net liquidation value

title: CSP Strategy Performance Report — Feb 9-19, 2026
summary:
Overall Performance: Exceptionally strong with 80% win rate and 39.6% average return per trade. Total P&L: +$1,758 across 10 trades.

Key Metrics

Average hold time: 0.7 days. Average premium captured on winners: 58.7%. Best trade: HL +$610 (87.1% return). Worst trade: NBIS -$192. Profit per day: $546.

Why It Worked

VIX at 20-23 created ideal conditions: high premiums to collect (inflated by fear), rapid IV compression after panic, and ultra-short holds capturing panic-to-relief whipsaws. Stock selection aligned with sectors receiving rotation inflows (materials, energy, industrials).

Sustainability Assessment

Current returns are 2-3x elevated by market conditions. When VIX normalises to 12-16, expect: average returns to halve (15-25%), hold times to extend to 2-3 days, premium captured to drop to 30-40%.
